title: Consultation: A vision for public health assessment for Canada’s future  - Canada.ca
content:
## Consultation: A vision for public health assessment for Canada’s future
From: Public Health Agency of Canada

## Current status: Closed
This consultation ran from November 20, 2023 to March 1, 2024.
We asked for input on the development of a vision for what public health assessment in Canada could look like by 2030. The project team is assessing feedback from the consultation, which will help to inform the vision.

## Who was the focus of this consultation
We engaged with stakeholders like :
- healthcare professionals
- academic and research professionals
- non-government health organizations
- community health worker or advocates
- government administrators or policy makers
- industry professionals in health-related fields
- First Nations, Inuit and Métis health-related organizations and groups
Members of the public with an interest in the subject matter could also participate.

## Themes and areas of focus
This survey touched on the following themes:
- Current or new health concerns that would benefit from being assessed.
- Envisioning new improvements to how risks and health are assessed.
- Evaluating existing public health assessment processes.
Explore the final report for this initiative:
- About the Vision 2030 initiative
Email: ppu-upp@phac-aspc.gc.ca
